response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 15+ years of broad full-stack and systems engineering experience, currently at Staff level at Google, with a strong track record across distributed, high-throughput services and developer tooling. However, the role's core mandate — owning experimentation frameworks, feature-flagging infrastructure, telemetry foundations, bias detection, and rollout-risk management — is entirely absent from the resume. There is no evidence of building or operating A/B testing platforms, experiment assignment/targeting systems, or growth measurement infrastructure at any point in his career, which are non-negotiable requirements at the Principal level for this position.
strengths: Demonstrated scale at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby operating microservices under millions of HTTP requests per second, showing real distributed-systems depth
Currently a Staff Engineer at Google contributing to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, signaling continued growth and relevance in a top-tier engineering environment
Broad polyglot background (TypeScript, C/C++, Java, D, Swift) and 15+ years across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems demonstrates strong fundamentals
concerns: Zero experimentation platform experience: no mention of A/B testing, feature flags, experiment assignment, rollout automation, or statistical guardrails anywhere in the resume
No growth engineering background: no evidence of working on conversion funnels, growth metrics, telemetry pipelines, or data-driven product measurement systems

Resume framing and formatting raise questions about IC6 readiness — the 'Founder, Amazon' entry appears to be a mislabeled open-source side project, and the current Google role description lacks the cross-team influence narrative expected at Principal/IC6 scope
